home.scanModeSub: Decode any QR / barcode and show its contents.
home.noConfigsSub: Add one with an API URL to start validating tickets.

history.clearConfirm: Delete scan history for %{scope}? This cannot be undone.

scan.isSetupLink: This is an Open Ticket Scanner setup link.

configure.invalidBody: This link or QR code doesn’t contain a valid configuration.
configure.duplicateLead: A configuration with this API URL already exists.
configure.reviewLead: Review this configuration before adding it.
configure.name: Name

configList.empty: No configurations yet. Add one below.

configEdit.name: Name
configEdit.scannerHint: Sent with each scan to identify this device/lane.
configEdit.continuousSub: Keep scanning hands-free, showing a brief result toast for each ticket.
configEdit.debounceHint: Ignore the same code if re-scanned within this time.
configEdit.invalidUrlBody: Enter a full http(s) URL to POST scans to.
configEdit.noFormatsBody: Select at least one code format to detect.

ticketScan.networkError: Network error — could not reach server

share.caption: Scan this with another device (Ticket configurations → Scan setup code), or share the link.
share.keyWarn: ⚠ The QR/link contains your API key — share it only with trusted devices.
share.keyOmitted: The key is omitted; enter it on each device after importing.

configureScan.rejected: Not a setup code — try again
configureScan.hint: Point at a configuration QR code

settings.vibrateSub: Haptic buzz mapped to valid / warning / rejected.
settings.beepSub: Play a short tone for each result (plays on silent too).
settings.deviceNote: The device ID and app version are sent with every ticket scan (ID in the body, version as the X-App-Version header).

about.intro: A fast, no-nonsense scanner for QR codes, barcodes and event tickets. Point your camera at a code to decode it, or validate tickets against your own API.
about.openSourceBody: Open Ticket Scanner is free and open source. Browse the code, report an issue, or contribute on GitHub.
about.developedByBody: Built and maintained by Fruitcake, a development agency from the Netherlands.
